props.name: rel
props.docs:
The relationship between the current document and the linked document.
supported values are `alternate` | `author` | `bookmark` | `external` | `help` | `license` | `next` | `nofollow` | `noopener` |
`noreferrer` | `prev` | `search` | `tag` | `_top`

props.name: target
props.docs:
Where to open the linked document.
supported values are `_blank`|`_self`|`_parent`|`_top`

The tooltip target selector which will be queried for in order to
position the tooltip
By default, it looks for a wz-tooltip-target tag under the tooltip element

Whether the tooltip opens by hover over the source element or click on it

By default, the tooltip opens on hover

props.name: checked
props.docs:
Whether the chip is checked.
When true, a check icon will appear.

props.name: controlled
props.docs:
Indicates whether the checked state of the chip is controlled from outside the component.
If true, changes to the `checked` property from within the component will not toggle the chip's checked state.
Instead, the parent component is responsible for updating the `checked` property.
If false, the chip's checked state will toggle internally when clicked.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: Whether the chip is disabled.

props.name: error
props.docs: Whether the chip in error state.

props.name: highlighted
props.docs: Whether the chip is currently highlighted.

props.name: isDragging
props.docs: Whether the chip is being dragged.

props.name: readOnly
props.docs: Whether the chip is read-only.

props.name: removable
props.docs:
Whether the chip can be removed.
When true, the chip will display an X icon for removal.

props.name: showCheckIconWhenChecked
props.docs: Whether to show a check icon when the chip is checked.

props.name: size
props.docs:
The size of the chip. (default: md)
Can be one of the following: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'

props.name: value
props.docs: The value of the component.

props.name: checked
props.docs: Whether this checkbox is true or false

props.name: disabled
props.docs: Whether this checkbox is disabled or not

props.name: hasError
props.docs: Whether this checkbox has an error

props.name: indeterminate
props.docs:
Whether this checkbox is indeterminate
read more at: go/indeterminate-checkbox

props.name: name
props.docs: The checkbox element name.

props.name: value
props.docs:
The checkbox element value.
Defaults to "on" to be compatible with native checkbox specs:
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/checkbox
"If the value attribute was omitted, the default value for the checkbox is on"

props.name: maxSelected
props.docs: The maximum amount of chips that can be simultaneously selected.

props.name: alignment
props.docs: Aligns the dialog's inner text, including `wz-dialog-header` and `wz-dialog-content`

props.name: dismissible
props.docs:
Whether the dialog is dismissible, meaning can it be closed without
using any of the provided controls.
If dismissible=true, the dialog can be closed by clicking on the
dark background or by clicking an 'X' icon on the top right of the dialog.

props.name: size
props.docs:
Dialog's width, according to the following specs:
xxs: 280px
xs: 320px
sm: 400px
lg: 560px
xl: 720px

events.docs: Dispatched after hiding the dialog
Dispatched after showing the dialog

styles.name: --wz-dialog-dismiss-button-fill
styles.docs: The dismiss button fill value. default: `#3c4043`

styles.name: --wz-dialog-dismiss-button-top
styles.docs: The dismiss button top value. default: 16px

styles.name: --wz-dialog-dismiss-button-width
styles.docs: The dismiss button width value. default: 24px

styles.name: --wz-dialog-height
styles.docs: The dialog height value. If not specified, the height is computed according to the content.

styles.name: --wz-dialog-width
styles.docs: The dialog width value, regardless the dialog size. If not specified, the width depends on the dialog size.

props.name: layout
props.docs:
Layout for the dialog controls.
If using "vertical" and the controls are of type Wz-Button,
They will render full width by default.

props.docs: Whether the menu is currently open or not.

props.docs: Whether the menu should open at the mouse click location.

props.name: upperMenu
props.docs:
Whether the menu should be forced to open upwards.
By default, the menu will check if it has enough space downwards, and if it does not, it will open upwards.
By passing "true" to this prop the menu will open upwards regardless to it's position.

events.docs: The event 'didUpdate' is fired after the component updates

The event 'menuToggled' is fired with an expanded boolean on menu toggle,
to notify other components when the menu's state is changing.

Whether or not to allow setting the value of the search to be a string that is not in the getItems.
Useful in case you want to redirect to multi-option list once the search is complete.

props.name: getItems
props.docs: A query function that has to be provided, in order for the autocomplete to show suggestions.

props.name: helperMessage
props.docs: The assistive text for the text input

props.name: isCaseSensitive
props.docs: Whether to respect case when matching query and options text on element blur.

props.docs: The label of the autocomplete input.

props.docs: The max length that the AutoComplete input allows.

props.docs: The placeholder text in the field while there is nothing written.

props.name: shouldDisplayDropdownSymbol
props.docs: Whether or not to display the dropdown symbol.

props.name: shouldMatchOnSecondaryText
props.docs:
Whether to match the query on the secondary text as well.
Doesn't affect prefix mode.

props.name: textChangedError
props.docs:
The function to be called when error occurs on filtering function.
By default, exposes the error reason.

props.name: values
props.docs:
Array of the selected Items. valuesChanged event won't be triggered when changing this prop.
Check `IAutocompleteItem` type on [wz-autocomplete component page](/?path=/docs/🧩-components-autocomplete--docs).

events.docs:
The event which will be triggered when the user changes the AutoComplete internal input.
The event detail is the updated input value (the user query).
The event which will be triggered when suggestion menu opens/closes.
The event which will be triggered when a value is checked/unchecked.

The event which will be triggered once an item is selected/removed by the user.
This event isn't triggered when changing the values prop.
The event detail is an object with the following type:
`{ values: IAutocompleteItem[]; added?: IAutocompleteItem; removed?: IAutocompleteItem[]; }`

props.name: value
props.docs:
The value to display.
If 'value <= 0' then it shows a red dot.
If '0 < value < 100' then it shows the value.
If 'value >= 100' then it shows the '99+' string.

Whether the current option should be highlighted,
e.g as a selected option or pre selected option (keyboard navigating)
in wz-select

The option clicked event is being fired on option click to notify
the parent wz-select component that an option was selected, and
its value.

props.docs: Whether to expand navigation drawer on hover in collapsed state

props.name: collapsedMode
props.docs:
Whether to display navigation drawer in collapsed mode relative to the page content
side: on the side, pushing the content
none: not to display

props.name: expandedMode
props.docs:
Whether to display navigation drawer in expanded mode relative to the page content
side: on the side, pushing the content
over: over the content with a backdrop behind

props.name: hoverExpandedMode

Whether to display navigation drawer in hover expanded mode relative to the page content
side: on the side, pushing the content
over: over the content with a backdrop behind

props.docs: Whether the navigation drawer is in expanded or collapsed state

props.name: isCollapsedLabeled
props.docs: Whether the navigation drawer in collapsed-labeled state

methods.name: toggleNavigationDrawer
methods.docs: Toggles the navigation drawer state - hides it if it's visible, or shows it if it's hidden

props.name: disabled
props.docs: Whether progressbar is disabled or not. (default: false)

props.name: message
props.docs:
The message which describes progressbar status.
When used with status it appears as error or warning

props.name: progress
props.docs:
The current progress. (default: 0)
Number in range 0-100

props.name: size
props.docs:
The size of the progressbar. (default: md)
Can be one of the following: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'

props.name: status
props.docs:
The status of the progressbar.
Can be one of the following: 'error' | 'warning'

slots.name: progressbar-tooltip

props.name: checked
props.docs: Whether this radio button is Checked or not

props.name: disabled
props.docs: Whether this radio button is disabled or not

props.name: hasError
props.docs: Whether this radio button has an error or not

props.name: name
props.docs: The name of the radio button group

props.name: value
props.docs: The value of the radio button

props.name: layout
props.docs:
The direction of the radio buttons
either 'horizontal' or 'vertical'

props.name: hideDelayMs
props.docs:
The milliseconds delay before closing the tooltip content
By default, it closes async with 0 timeout

props.name: targetSelector
props.docs:
The tooltip target selector which will be queried for in order to
position the tooltip
By default, it looks for a wz-tooltip-target tag under the tooltip element

props.name: trigger
props.docs:
Whether the tooltip opens by hover over the source element or click on it
Can be one of the following: 'hover' | 'click'
By default, the tooltip opens on hover

events.docs: Fired when the tooltip has closed.
Fired when the tooltip has opened.

props.docs: The selected value of the select component.

props.name: align
props.docs: Whether the snackbar is at the center of the screen.

props.name: closeAutomatically
props.docs: Whether the snackbar closes automatically after the delay configured in closeDelay.

props.name: closeButton
props.docs: Whether the snackbar has a close button.

props.name: closeDelay
props.docs: The delay in milliseconds before the snackbar will close automatically.

props.name: position
props.docs: Whether the snackbar is at the top or bottom of the screen.

props.name: shouldPositionBasedOnWindowSize
props.docs:
Whether to position the snackbar based on window size.

When set to true, the align and position props will position
the snackbar based on the window as its container.

When set to false, the default container will be used.
(If any parent element has a transform css rule, it will be considered as the
default container for the snackbar with css position set to "fixed").

components.overview:
A wrapper element for the snackbar's actions.
Used to always position the actions at the end* of the snackbar.
End according to the context direction- right if ltr, left if rtl.
components.docs:
A wrapper element for the snackbar's actions.
Used to always position the actions at the end* of the snackbar.
End according to the context direction- right if ltr, left if rtl.

props.name: ratingScore
props.docs:
Rating Score.
A number. The number will be rounded
to the closest number of stars between 0 and 5 (inc.)
with 0.5 intervals

props.name: mode
props.docs:
Star mode. (default: FULL)
Can be one of the following: 'full' | 'empty' | 'half'

props.name: color
props.docs:
The color of the chip. (default: grey)
Can be one of the following: 'grey' | 'red' | 'yellow' | 'green' | 'outline'

props.name: hasDot
props.docs: Whether the chip shows a dot icon.

props.name: isDragging
props.docs: Whether the chip is currently being dragged.

props.name: size
props.docs:
The size of the chip. (default: md)
Can be one of the following: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'

props.name: disabled
props.docs: Whether this tab is disabled or not.

props.name: isActive
props.docs: Whether this tab is active or not.

props.name: keyboardFocused
props.docs: Whether this tab is focused by the keyboard or not.

props.name: busy
props.docs: Whether to render the spinner and disable the content

props.name: caption
props.docs:
The heading text of the table
If caption exist, the wz-table will render
a sub-component wz-table-controller with the caption

props.name: dense
props.docs:
Whether the table is dense or not
Affects the height of the rows

props.name: filterItems
props.docs: List of possible items to filter table by

props.name: hasExternalPagination
props.docs:
Whether the table has the pagination element external to it

If false, the table is rounded at the bottom, and isn't otherwise

props.name: manualSort
props.docs:
Whether the sorting is manual

automatic sorting - sorting is done within the component.

manual sorting - no sorting is done upon a sorting button click
(should be done outside by manipulating the data when a sorting event is triggered).

props.name: searchPlaceholder
props.docs: controls the search query placeholder string

props.name: searchType
props.docs: Defines what type of search to use

props.name: searchValue
props.docs: search input value

props.name: searchable
props.docs: defines whether to show the search or not

events.docs: Event that fires upon every change in any filter selection
An event for table search

An event for table sorting requests (triggered regardless of manualSort property state).
should be used for manual sorting, and to know when a sorting is triggered.

props.name: caption
props.docs: The heading text of the controller

props.name: searchValue
props.docs: search input value

docsTags.name: note
docsTags.text: consumes/emits "page-number-index" as "index" i.e. first page starts from 0

props.name: currentPageIndex
props.docs: The current page

props.name: hasMorePages
props.docs:
Whether new pages can be loaded.
If defined, plus icon near last page button will be rendered.

props.name: mode
props.docs:
Defines how the pagination events and row re-renders are handled:
- `auto`: the component handles page and arrow click events
   internally and mutates the rows DOM automatically;
- `manual`: the component only emits an event on page change for
   the consumer to change the rows UI.

props.name: nextButtonDisabled
props.docs: Whether the next button is disabled, e.g. if there's no more next data.

props.name: pageSize
props.docs: Rows per page.

props.name: rangeSummaryText
props.docs: Custom text for displayed range.

props.name: totalCount
props.docs:
Total count of the results.
If defined, arrow and index page buttons will be rendered.

methods.name: setTotalCount
methods.docs:
The `setTotalCount` is called from the parent wz-table component
on initial load when the pagination mode is `manual`.
wz-table calculates the count of all the wz-tr elements present
in the DOM and sets it for the wz-table-page-controls component.

events.docs: The `pageChanged` event is fired when either a page or an arrow button is clicked.

The `visibleRowsChanged` event is being fired on page change to notify
the parent wz-table component that a page was changed, and
currently displayed wz-tr elements should be replaced
with the elements starting with `from` and ending
with `to` (not inclusive) if the mode is `auto`.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: tab-label-{index} - Defines the label for the tab number `index`

props.name: fixed
props.docs: Whether the tabs width is fixed or not.

slots.name: tab-label-{index}
slots.docs: Defines the label for the tab number `index`

props.name: contentType
props.docs:
The type of the table cell inner content
Affects its horizontal alignment

props.name: autocomplete
props.docs:
Whether or not an input field should have autocomplete enabled.
Can be one of the following: 'on' | 'off'

props.name: autofocus
props.docs: Whether this text input should be autofocused when the page loads.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: Whether this text input is disabled or not

props.name: displayMaxlength
props.docs: Whether or not to display the value length / maxlength counter.

props.name: errorMessage
props.docs: The error message for error state

props.name: helperMessage
props.docs: The assistive text for the text input

props.name: label
props.docs: The label of the input

props.name: max
props.docs: The max value for the number input

props.name: maxlength
props.docs: The max length that the input allows

props.name: min
props.docs: The min value for the number input

props.name: name

props.name: placeholder
props.docs: The text to be shown in the placeholder for the text input

props.name: readonly
props.docs: Whether this text input is readonly or not

props.name: selectionEnd
props.docs: The end index of the selected text

props.name: selectionStart
props.docs: The beginning index of the selected text

props.name: size
props.docs:
The size of the text input. (default: md)
Can be one of the following: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'

props.name: step
props.docs: The granularity that the number input value must adhere to

props.name: subtitle
props.docs: The subtitle of the input

props.name: successMessage
props.docs: The success message for success state

props.name: type
props.docs: The type of the text input.

props.name: value
props.docs: The value of the text input

methods.name: select
methods.docs: Selects all text in the input

methods.name: setSelectionRange

complexType.signature: (start: number | null, end: number | null, direction?: "forward" | "backward" | "none") => Promise<void>

parameters.name: start
parameters.docs:
The 0-based index of the first selected character.
An index greater than the length of the element's value is treated as pointing to the end of the value.

parameters.name: end
parameters.docs:
The 0-based index of the character after the last selected character.
An index greater than the length of the element's value is treated as pointing to the end of the value.
If selectionEnd is less than selectionStart, then both are treated as the value of selectionEnd.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.docs: (Optional) A string indicating the direction in which the selection is considered to have been performed.

methods.signature: setSelectionRange(start: number | null, end: number | null, direction?: "forward" | "backward" | "none") => Promise<void>

parameters.name: start
parameters.docs:
The 0-based index of the first selected character.
An index greater than the length of the element's value is treated as pointing to the end of the value.

parameters.name: end
parameters.docs:
The 0-based index of the character after the last selected character.
An index greater than the length of the element's value is treated as pointing to the end of the value.
If selectionEnd is less than selectionStart, then both are treated as the value of selectionEnd.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.docs: (Optional) A string indicating the direction in which the selection is considered to have been performed.

methods.docs: Sets the start and end positions of the current text selection

The comparing function used to sort the rows by this header
In case a a function is not present, an alphanumerical comparison will be used

props.name: contentType

The type of the table cell inner content
Affects its horizontal alignment

props.name: sortable
props.docs: Whether this header is sortable or not

props.name: sortingKey
props.docs:
The key which will be sent as part of the sorting event
In case a sortingKey is not present, the title will be used as the sorting key

components.overview: Switch between states by clicking on the toggle button.
components.docs: Switch between states by clicking on the toggle button.

props.name: checked
props.docs: Whether this toggle switch is true or false

props.name: disabled
props.docs: Whether this toggle switch is disabled or not

props.name: name
props.docs: Native hidden checkbox's name prop.

props.name: reversed
props.docs: If true, render the label before the toggle-switch

props.name: value
props.docs: Native hidden checkbox's value prop.

props.name: hideDelayMs
props.docs:
The milliseconds delay before closing the tooltip content
By default, it closes async with 0 timeout

props.name: targetSelector
props.docs:
The tooltip target selector which will be queried for in order to
position the tooltip
By default, it looks for a wz-tooltip-target tag under the tooltip element

props.name: trigger
props.docs:
Whether the tooltip opens by hover over the source element or click on it
Can be one of the following: 'hover' | 'click'
By default, the tooltip opens on hover

methods.name: toggle
methods.signature: toggle({ target }: MouseEvent & { target: HTMLElement; }) => Promise<void>

components.overview: What to render as a pop-up tooltip
components.docs: What to render as a pop-up tooltip

props.name: position
props.docs:
Whether the tooltip should be open to right/left/bottom/up
Can be one of the following: 'right' | 'left' | 'bottom' | up
By default, the tooltip opens to bottom

components.overview: Where to hover/click to open tooltip
components.docs: Where to hover/click to open tooltip

props.name: alarming

Whether to show all the content.

props.docs:
Maximum number of characters before content will be truncated.
If not set content will not be truncated

events.docs: Triggered when expanded state is changed

slots.name: ellipsis

slots.name: showLess

export type AutocompleteMode =
  (typeof AUTOCOMPLETE_MODE)[keyof typeof AUTOCOMPLETE_MODE];

export type EnableNewItems =
  (typeof ENABLE_NEW_ITEMS)[keyof typeof ENABLE_NEW_ITEMS];
